instruction:
Consider the function f(x)=\frac{x}{\sqrt{16-x^2}} a) Determine the domain of the function. Note: Write the answer in interval notation. If the answer involves more than one interval write the intervals separated by the union symbol, U. If needed enter $\infty$ as infinity and $-\infty$ as-infinity. Domain=[ANS]
b) Find the vertical asymptote(s). If there is more than one vertical asymptote give a list of the $x$-values separated by commas. If there are no vertical asymptotes type in None. $x=$ [ANS]
